Text: Company and investors don't think a physical release is important.

Options.Text: Yeah it is! Plus, we are going to support them!
Options.Twitter: SuperCool is now officialy supporting physical release unlike other big games companies. Big W.

Options.Text: There exist people that want them, we maintain neutral tho.
Options.Twitter: can't wait to have a new physical game next to my supercool collection :D

Options.Text: They are probably right, we need to save money.

Text: Investors are willing to give a ton of money with inclusion of AI.

Options.Text: Let's attract more investors with AI
Options.Twitter: sorry but i dont understand supercools new game... everything seems to focused on ai for some reason rather that the game itself? I will not be buying this.

Options.Text: We will have to use AI then.
Options.Twitter: I HATE AI!! AND I HATE THATS BEING USED ON MY NEXT FAV GAME!!!!! RAHHHHHHH!!1111!!!!11 >:CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC CC C. *cries*

Options.Text: I don't think that's ideal for this game.
No! We are totally agaisnt that!
Options.Twitter: You all might be excited about SuperCool not using AI for once, but what you don't get this was to get investor's back. A very brave move, but i'm worried about what they are gonna do without their support.

Text: Popularised fangames and artwork are becoming a threat for our Intellectual Property.

Options.Text: In order to not lose property we must take down these.
Options.Twitter: Thank you SuperCool. Thank you for making me excited about one of your products and promoting it to fucking sue me. You clearly treat your community very well. Fuck everyone.

Options.Text: We must send warnings to the creators to prevent these.
Options.Twitter: Uhh Guys WTF. I just received a warning from SuperCool cuz i was using their characters... Dude, This is the OPPOSITE OF COOL.

Options.Text: We'll leave it as is for now.
Free publicity for us, let's encourage them!
Options.Twitter: hell yea supercool new fangame coming soon 👀

Text: Company suggests adding microtransactions as an additional way of revenue.

Options.Text: It would be best to maximize our earnings.
Options.Twitter: This game's microtransaction are so annoying. They literally pop up every few seconds, UGH.

Options.Text: It will help cover the production costs.
Microtransaction aren't essential to this game.

Text: Marketing department is considering sponsoring streamers for playing the game.

Options.Text: Contact much popular streamers as possible.
Options.Twitter: OMG MY FAV STREAMER JUST PLAYED THIS SUPER COOL GAME I NEED TO PLAY IT WHEN IT RELEASES [b]100%!!!!!

Options.Text: Only contact a few and small streamers.
Options.Twitter: TBH i've never received a sponsorship but this game really seems nice you guys should check it out ♥️

Options.Text: No thanks.

Text: We should need an anti-cheat to prevent hacking cases in the game.

Options.Text: Implement the most secure anti-cheat possible.
Options.Twitter: WTF is this game fucking warzone now???? [b]wdym change BIOS SETTINGS?!?![/b] this anticheat is TOO extreme.

Options.Text: Have a regular anticheat
The game will be fine without anti-cheat.
Options.Twitter: Guys. Do not play SuperCool's new game. I just got hacked from it, all servers are infested with hackers. They won't care for applying security measures, this fucking sucks.

Text: This videogame company is giving us 30000$ if our game becomes exclusive to their console.

Options.Text: Deal!! We need the money!!!
Options.Twitter: So sad I don't have a console to play Super Cool's new game :-(...

Options.Text: No thanks.

Text: Analytics recommend collecting player's data as an alternative source of money.

Options.Text: Collect and sell player's data to third parties.
Options.Twitter: This is [b]DISGUSTING[/b]. SuperCool is now SELLING YOUR DATA if you play their new game. I don't want to be part of such evil squeme.

Options.Text: Opt out of collecting data.

Text: It's suggested to enter the stock-exchange for gaining much more investors.

Options.Text: Enter the stock exchange.
Options.Twitter: what have games turned into nowadays? game companies are all entering the stock exchange blinded by investors... this is why i prefer indies.

Options.Text: Do not enter.

Text: The semester stadistics are lower than expected, threatening our investor leadership.

Options.Text: 'Adjust' (inflate) stadistics (aka. lie to get more investors).
Options.Twitter: Investor here. SuperCool's semester stadistics seem suspiciously high, considering their past actions...

Options.Text: Release them as is.
Release little to no stadistics.
Options.Twitter: anyone knows why supercool are trying to hide their expected sales?

Text: A major snack brand wants to collaborate with our game in exchange of exposure.

Options.Text: Let's do it!
Options.Twitter: what is the point of having a soda-branded skin on my game

Options.Text: Reject sponsorship.

Text: In-game ads are suggested to remedy costs.

Options.Text: Let's do it!
Options.Twitter: which fucking big-company game on PC has ads. im not playing this shit just for you pigs to get money out of me.

Options.Text: Actually that idea is not that good.
